policy_statement: Adopt a citywide climate protection vision to reach zero net greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 and prepare for likely climate change impacts (Resolution 31312).
verbatim_text:
In 2011, recognizing that the Kyoto Protocol goal is just the first step forward in reducing climate pollution, the Mayor and City Council adopted a bold climate protection vision for our community (through Resolution 31312):
- Reach Zero Net Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions by 2050
- Prepare for the likely impacts of climate change
extraction_rationale: Formal, adopted City vision (via resolution) with a quantified long-term target (zero net GHG by 2050) and a defined adaptation objective.

policy_statement: Monitor and report progress on the 2013 Climate Action Plan by annually reporting implementation of 2015/2030 actions, reporting outcome indicators every other year, and producing a community GHG inventory every three years (including 2012 and 2015 inventories).
verbatim_text:
The Office of Sustainability & Environment will be responsible for monitoring and reporting on the progress of the 2013 CAP's targets and actions on the following schedule:
- Implementation of 2015 Actions & 2030 Actions: Annually.
- Climate Action Outcome Indicators: Every other year, or as data is available.
- Local Community GHG Emissions: Every three years, including inventories for 2012 & 2015.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment assigning responsibility and a defined monitoring/reporting schedule.

policy_statement: Update the Climate Action Plan over time by adding new short-term actions and revisiting the suite of 2030 actions in 2016.
verbatim_text: This 2013 Climate Action Plan is intended to evolve over time... the City will update the Climate Action Plan with new short-term actions and will revisit the suite of 2030 actions in 2016.
extraction_rationale: Concrete planning commitment with a specified future review year (2016).

policy_statement: Set 2030 transportation outcome targets including: 82% reduction in passenger vehicle emissions, 20% reduction in vehicle miles traveled, and 75% reduction in GHG emissions intensity per mile, from a 2008 baseline.
verbatim_text: The City's 2030 goals are to reduce emissions from passenger vehicle transportation by 82%, vehicle miles traveled by 20%, and emissions per mile traveled by 75% from a 2008 baseline.
section_header: Transportation + Land Use — How much will these actions reduce GHG emissions?
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified targets with baseline year, meeting the quantifiable target criterion.

policy_statement: Set 2030 building energy outcome targets to reduce commercial building energy use by 10%, residential energy use by 20%, reduce GHG intensity of all fuels by 25%, and reduce total building energy GHG emissions by 39%.
verbatim_text: The City's 2030 goals are to reduce energy use in commercial buildings by 10% and residential buildings by 20%, and reduce the GHG intensity of all fuels by 25%... meet our overall 2030 target to reduce total building energy GHG emissions by 39%.
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified sector targets with a deadline (2030).

policy_statement: Set waste outcome targets including achieving a 70% diversion rate by 2022 and reducing methane emissions from landfill by 50% by 2020.
verbatim_text:
| Recycling & Composting             | Waste diverted from landfill to recycling and composting | 70% diversion rate by 2022                 |
| Collection, Processing, & Disposal | Methane emissions from landfill                          | 50% reduction in methane emissions by 2020 |
extraction_rationale: Quantified targets with years for diversion and methane reductions.

policy_statement: Transportation Choices (umbrella): implement actions across funding, infrastructure & service, demand management, and vehicle fuels & technologies to expand transit, walking, bicycling and related services for a low-carbon transportation system.
verbatim_text:
The Transportation & Land Use actions are organized as follows:
## TRANSPORTATION CHOICES:
Funding
Transportation Infrastructure & Service
Transportation Demand Management
Vehicle Fuels & Technologies
extraction_rationale: Introduces a named action grouping with multiple sub-categories and associated numbered actions below.

policy_statement: Funding (Transportation Choices): secure adequate funding to implement the Transit, Bicycle, Pedestrian and Freight Master Plans and meet maintenance needs.
verbatim_text:
## Funding
## 2030 Vision
- Seattle has adequate funding to fully implement the Transit, Bicycle, Pedestrian and Freight Master Plans, and meet maintenance needs.
extraction_rationale: A defined action category with numbered actions listed beneath it (2015 actions).

policy_statement: Renew and extend the time frame of the Bridging the Gap levy and prioritize investments in transit, pedestrian, and bicycling improvements and system maintenance.
verbatim_text:
## Actions to Implement by 2015
1. Renew and extend the time frame of the Bridging the Gap levy and prioritize investments in transit, pedestrian, and bicycling improvements and system maintenance.
parent_policy_name: Funding (Transportation Choices): secure adequate funding to implement the Transit, Bicycle, Pedestrian and Freight Master Plans and meet maintenance needs.
extraction_rationale: Specific near-term funding action item with a named levy and investment priorities.

policy_statement: Secure local or transit agency authority to levy a motor vehicle excise tax (MVET) at the City or County level.
verbatim_text:
## Actions to Implement by 2015
2. Secure local or transit agency authority to levy a motor vehicle excise tax (MVET) at the City or County level.
parent_policy_name: Funding (Transportation Choices): secure adequate funding to implement the Transit, Bicycle, Pedestrian and Freight Master Plans and meet maintenance needs.
extraction_rationale: Concrete policy mechanism (tax authority) explicitly stated.

policy_statement: Work with regional and state partners to adopt a transportation funding strategy, including mechanisms such as a city development authority, a tax on unpaid off-street parking in commercial areas, and comprehensive congestion pricing on limited access highways (and potentially arterials).
verbatim_text:
## Actions to Implement by 2015
3. Work with regional and state partners to adopt a funding strategy to meet current and future transportation needs including mechanisms such as:
- a) A city development authority* ... forming public private partnerships in order to use district-based funding mechanisms...
- b) A tax on unpaid off-street parking* in commercial areas...
- c) A comprehensive system of congestion pricing on all limited access highways in Central Puget Sound and potentially non-highway arterials.

*State legislative action required.
parent_policy_name: Funding (Transportation Choices): secure adequate funding to implement the Transit, Bicycle, Pedestrian and Freight Master Plans and meet maintenance needs.
extraction_rationale: Specified set of funding mechanisms and partnerships; includes pricing and institutional mechanisms.

policy_statement: Economic Signals (umbrella): implement road pricing and parking management strategies to reduce GHG emissions and congestion and generate revenue for transportation choices.
verbatim_text:
## ECONOMIC SIGNALS:
Road Pricing
Parking Management
extraction_rationale: Named action grouping with two subcomponents and numbered actions below.

policy_statement: Road Pricing (Economic Signals): research and pursue authorization and pilots for comprehensive road pricing to reduce emissions and fund multimodal transportation.
verbatim_text:
## Road Pricing
... generate substantial revenue to fund the transportation system.

## Actions to Implement by 2015
1. Research the benefits of pricing policies...

## Actions to Implement by 2030
1. Secure authorization to implement a comprehensive system of road pricing*...
extraction_rationale: Named category with distinct 2015 and 2030 numbered actions.

policy_statement: Research pricing policies' benefits and equity impacts by examining experiences of other communities.
verbatim_text:
## Actions to Implement by 2015
1. Research the benefits of pricing policies on climate protection, transportation and community goals (e.g. reduced congestion, improved air quality, revenue generation) and their potential social equity impacts and solutions by examining the experience of other communities.
parent_policy_name: Road Pricing (Economic Signals): research and pursue authorization and pilots for comprehensive road pricing to reduce emissions and fund multimodal transportation.
extraction_rationale: Concrete research commitment tied to pricing policy design and equity.

policy_statement: Secure authorization for comprehensive road pricing on limited access highways in Central Puget Sound, ensure authority to set objectives and rates, and dedicate revenues to multimodal projects by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Actions to Implement by 2030
1. Secure authorization to implement a comprehensive system of road pricing* on all limited access highways in Central Puget Sound. Work to ensure the region has the authority* to set transportation planning objectives and rates to meet the objectives. Ensure the region* can dedicate road pricing revenues to multimodal transportation projects.

*State legislative action required.
parent_policy_name: Road Pricing (Economic Signals): research and pursue authorization and pilots for comprehensive road pricing to reduce emissions and fund multimodal transportation.
extraction_rationale: Specific authorization and revenue dedication commitments with clear scope.

policy_statement: Evaluate road pricing on non-highway arterials and develop a pilot to test congestion and emissions reduction and funding potential by 2030.
verbatim_text:

2. Evaluate road pricing opportunities on non-highway arterials and develop a pilot project to test whether road pricing can help reduce congestion while also reducing emissions and providing funding for transportation choices.
parent_policy_name: Road Pricing (Economic Signals): research and pursue authorization and pilots for comprehensive road pricing to reduce emissions and fund multimodal transportation.
extraction_rationale: Explicit evaluation plus pilot development commitment.

## Actions to Implement by 2015
2. Develop a comprehensive adaptation strategy that integrates the City's planning efforts across all relevant departments and considers both the cost of implementing actions to improve our ability to adapt and the potential cost of inaction. Engage residents in developing the strategy.
parent_policy_name: Assessment & Planning (Adaptation): conduct citywide climate impact assessment and develop a comprehensive adaptation strategy, and apply sea level rise planning guidance to impacted capital projects (by 2015).
section_header: Preparing for Climate Change — ASSESSMENT & PLANNING
extraction_rationale: Concrete strategy development commitment with public engagement and cost considerations.

policy_statement: Apply the Sea Level Rise Planning Guidance for Capital Projects methodology to projects projected to be impacted by sea level rise (by 2015).
verbatim_text:
## Actions to Implement by 2015
3. Apply the planning methodology detailed in the City of Seattle Sea Level Rise Planning Guidance for Capital Projects to projects projected to be impacted by sea level rise.
parent_policy_name: Assessment & Planning (Adaptation): conduct citywide climate impact assessment and develop a comprehensive adaptation strategy, and apply sea level rise planning guidance to impacted capital projects (by 2015).
section_header: Preparing for Climate Change — ASSESSMENT & PLANNING
extraction_rationale: Specific methodological requirement for capital project planning.
